text.title: growth-conversion-optimization Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for growth-conversion-optimization. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Follow the research-before-testing rule: run heatmaps, session recordings, and 5+ user interviews before writing a single A/B test hypothesis.
rule.rationale: Random changes produce random results. Research anchors hypotheses to real user friction, which lifts win rate from ~30% to ~50% per test.

rule.statement: Use the PIE framework to prioritize: score each opportunity on Potential (impact headroom), Importance (traffic volume), and Ease (implementation cost), then divide by 3. Test highest-scoring opportunities first.
rule.rationale: High-traffic pages with large drop-offs and low-effort fixes return value fastest; PIE prevents teams from polishing low-traffic pages.

rule.statement: Establish baseline metrics per funnel stage before starting any CRO work: landing page bounce rate target below 50%, signup form completion above 30%, pricing CTA click rate above 5%, checkout completion above 60%, visitor-to-customer 1-5%.
rule.rationale: Without baselines, test results have no reference frame and post-test "lift" claims are unverifiable.

rule.statement: Every test hypothesis must follow this template: "We believe that [change] will cause [effect] because [rationale]. We will measure this by [metric] and consider it successful if [target]."
rule.rationale: Pre-registration prevents post-hoc rationalization. Hypotheses without explicit success criteria inflate false-positive rates.

rule.statement: Do not declare a winner until: 100+ conversions per variant, 2+ weeks of runtime covering weekly cycles, and 95%+ statistical significance. Never peek at results before the minimum sample is reached.
rule.rationale: Stopping early at ~80% confidence inflates the false-positive rate from 5% to ~30%, polluting the learning library with phantom wins.

rule.statement: Run a 30-day holdback cell (95% rollout + 5% control) after shipping winners to monitor novelty regression.
rule.rationale: Winners often regress 30+ days post-launch; holdback cells catch regressions before they fully erode the gain.

rule.statement: Keep traffic assignment user-id-hashed, not session-based, and monitor for test pollution: bot traffic, consent-banner disruption, and cross-test contamination before calling results.
rule.rationale: Session-based assignment allows the same user to see both variants; bot traffic and consent banners silently inflate or deflate exposure counts.

rule.statement: Document every test result — winner, loser, or inconclusive — with: name, date, hypothesis, change, metric result, lift percentage, what generalizes, what does not, and next test idea.
rule.rationale: Teams that maintain a learning library double their annual win rate by avoiding repeated experiments and building on prior knowledge.
